html:
[605]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其技素平平 - An archaic way of saying 'usually average' or 'never outstanding.'
  • 斟酌但作水墨而已 - '斟酌' here may mean 'measured' or 'calculated,' but the context suggests he was being stingy or not fully committed.
  • 衣冠畧不设 - '畧不设' is an elliptical expression meaning 'did not even set up' or 'did not even dress.'
  • 霑受香火 - A standard expression referring to deities receiving worship and offerings.
  • 不为人所礼 - A passive construction meaning 'not respected by people.'
  • 藻饰 - Refers to the act of embellishing or decorating.
  • 称遂求者接踵 - A poetic expression meaning his fame spread.
  • 嫁女文绣只以画代之 - A metaphor for the high value placed on his paintings.
  • 遭疫疠无一家不病 - A common expression used in classical Chinese to describe a plague.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 城隍祠 - A temple dedicated to the City God (城隍), a deity in Chinese folk religion who protects the city and its people.
  • 绍兴中事 - An event from the Shaoxing period. Shaoxing (绍兴) is the era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song (1131–1162).
  • 金箔五采 - Refers to the materials used in the painting—gold leaf and colored pigments.
  • 文绣 - Refers to the luxurious fabric used in traditional Chinese weddings.

Proper Nouns

  • 胡一 - The painter's name.
  • 城隍 - A deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 绍兴 - Era name of Emperor Gaozong of the Song dynasty.

[606]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 凌二赌博 - Title describing the main character, Ling Er, and his primary activity, gambling.
  • 世世农业 - Means 'generations of farming,' indicating the family's traditional occupation.
  • 翁之次子 - Refers to 'the second son of the old man,' specifying the character's position in the family.
  • 独嗜赌博 - Means 'only addicted to gambling,' highlighting the character's unique trait.
  • 虽日挞不悛 - Means 'even though he was beaten daily, he did not reform,' describing his stubbornness.
  • 遇一客言能卜筮 - Means 'met a guest who said he could tell fortunes,' introducing a new character with supernatural abilities.
  • 以一神像画卷并一香炉自随 - Means 'carrying with him a scroll painting of a deity and an incense burner,' describing the guest's possessions.
  • 毎事必祝 - Means 'he would always pray for every matter,' indicating the guest's religious practices.
  • 凌子往扣 - Means 'Ling Er went to inquire,' showing the character's action.
  • 某日胜负 - Means 'the outcome of a certain day,' referring to the gambling results.
  • 客曰今夕胜五百钱盈数即止不可过也 - Means 'the guest said that he would win five hundred coins tonight, and when the amount was reached, he should stop and not exceed it,' providing instructions.
  • 已而诣山寺 - Means 'afterward, he went to a mountain temple,' describing a change in location.
  • 从其徒夜赌博 - Means 'he gambled with his disciples at night,' indicating the setting and participants.
  • 果得钱如数 - Means 'he indeed won the amount of money as predicted,' confirming the guest's accuracy.
  • 即再谒客致谢而举所赢买酒纵饮 - Means 'he then went to see the guest again, thanked him, and spent the winnings on buying wine to drink freely,' describing his actions.
  • 伺客醉卧负其两物 - Means 'he waited until the guest was drunk and asleep, then took the two items,' showing his dishonest behavior.
  • 客归不敢访寻 - Means 'the guest returned and dared not look for them,' indicating the consequences of his actions.
  • 狼狈而去 - Means 'he left in a disheveled state,' describing his departure.
  • 因设誓俟满五百千当整治生涯不复仍旧习 - Means 'he then took an oath that once he had accumulated five thousand coins, he would improve his life and not return to his old habits,' setting a goal.
  • 凡数年贮储过半而二亲继亡 - Means 'after several years, he had saved more than half of the amount, but his parents passed away in succession,' indicating a change in circumstances.
  • 殡葬之费于是乎出为之荡然 - Means 'the expenses for funerals came out of this, and he was left with nothing,' describing the financial impact.
  • 拘鞫囹圄尽耗其资乃获明白 - Means 'he was imprisoned and all his wealth was spent, but he eventually cleared his name,' showing the legal and financial consequences.
  • 迄于三既及四十八万矣 - Means 'by then, he had reached forty-eight thousand coins,' indicating his financial progress.
  • 一夕遭火悉为煨烬 - Means 'one night, a fire destroyed everything,' describing a catastrophic event.
  • 是时凌年过五十无复可营 - Means 'at that time, Ling Er was over fifty and had nothing left to do,' indicating his age and situation.
  • 既死其别子差能自立尝为毒蛇齧右手自断其臂得不死今犹在云 - Means 'after he died, his other son managed to support himself; he once was bitten by a venomous snake on his right hand and cut off his arm to survive; he is still alive,' providing a final update on the family.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 浮梁西村 - Refers to a village in the west of Fuliang, a county in modern-day Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 卜筮 - Refers to fortune-telling practices, which were common in ancient China.
  • 神像画卷 - Refers to a scroll painting of a deity, often used in religious rituals.
  • 香炉 - Refers to an incense burner, used in religious ceremonies.
  • 山寺 - Refers to a mountain temple, a common setting for spiritual and religious activities.
  • 山寺从其徒夜赌博 - Describes a gambling session at a mountain temple, which was a common social activity in ancient China.
  • 四十八万 - Refers to a large sum of money, indicating significant wealth.
  • 毒蛇齧右手自断其臂 - Describes a dramatic act of self-sacrifice, which was sometimes seen in ancient Chinese literature as a sign of extreme desperation or heroism.

Proper Nouns

  • 凌二 - The main character, a gambler.
  • 浮梁西村 - The setting, a village in the west of Fuliang, Jiangxi Province.
  • 山寺 - A mountain temple, a common setting for spiritual and religious activities.
  • 四十八万 - A large sum of money, indicating significant wealth.
  • 毒蛇 - A venomous snake, a common symbol of danger and death in ancient Chinese literature.
